name: Virtual Synchronization
description: Assemble data on demand without migration - data stays where it is

content.title: Virtual Synchronization
content.content:
# Virtual Synchronization

MeshWeaver assembles data on demand without requiring data migration. Your data stays where it is - MeshWeaver connects it.

## The Problem with Traditional ETL

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph "Traditional Approach"
        A[Source 1] -->|Copy| D[Data Warehouse]
        B[Source 2] -->|Copy| D
        C[Source 3] -->|Copy| D
    end
```

**Issues:**
- Data is stale by design (copied periodically)
- Storage duplication increases costs
- Complex ETL pipelines to maintain
- Changes propagate slowly

## Virtual Synchronization Approach

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph "MeshWeaver Approach"
        A[Source 1] <-->|Connect| D[MeshWeaver]
        B[Source 2] <-->|Connect| D
        C[Source 3] <-->|Connect| D
    end
```

**Benefits:**
- Data accessed at source - always current
- No storage duplication
- Simple connection, not complex pipelines
- Changes available immediately

## How It Works

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant AI as AI Agent
    participant Mesh as MeshWeaver
    participant S1 as Claims DB
    participant S2 as Contract System
    participant S3 as Document Store

    AI->>Mesh: Need context for claim CLM-123
    
    par Parallel Fetch
        Mesh->>S1: Get claim data
        Mesh->>S2: Get related contract
        Mesh->>S3: Get attached documents
    end
    
    S1->>Mesh: Claim details
    S2->>Mesh: Contract terms
    S3->>Mesh: Document metadata
    
    Mesh->>AI: Assembled context (live data)
```

## Non-Invasive Architecture

MeshWeaver connects to existing systems without requiring changes:

| Source Type | Connection Method | Changes Required |
|-------------|------------------|------------------|
| SQL Database | Read connection | None |
| REST API | API integration | None |
| Document Store | Storage connector | None |
| Legacy System | Adapter layer | Minimal |

## Data Stays Where It Is

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Your Infrastructure"
        A[SQL Server]
        B[Document Management]
        C[Email System]
        D[Cloud Storage]
    end

    subgraph "MeshWeaver Layer"
        E[Virtual Data Mesh]
    end

    A <-.->|Read/Write| E
    B <-.->|Read/Write| E
    C <-.->|Read| E
    D <-.->|Read/Write| E
```

Bidirectional arrows show MeshWeaver can both read and write back to source systems.

## Comparison

| Aspect | Traditional ETL | Virtual Sync |
|--------|----------------|---------------|
| Data freshness | Minutes to hours stale | Real-time |
| Storage cost | 2x or more duplication | No duplication |
| Implementation | Complex pipelines | Simple connections |
| Maintenance | High ongoing effort | Minimal |
| Time to value | Months | Days to weeks |

## Benefits for AI

- **Fresh context**: AI always works with current data
- **Complete picture**: Assemble data from multiple sources
- **Write-back**: AI-extracted data flows back to source systems
- **No sync issues**: Single source of truth maintained
